metadata.scientific_name: Traceable Human State Inference Under Partial Biological Observability
metadata.core_rule: Outputs are traceable hidden-state hypotheses with uncertainty, missing-data notes, guardrails, and next-best measurements. Never a diagnosis.

nodes.label: HPA Axis / Circadian Disruption
nodes.description: Flattened or phase-shifted cortisol awakening response consistent with HPA dysregulation.

nodes.label: Pem Not Normal Fatigue
nodes.description: Post-exertional malaise (PEM) is a specific, delayed, exertion-triggered worsening distinct from normal post-exertional fatigue.

nodes.label: Post-Exertional Malaise (Objective Branch)
nodes.description: Objective performance decrement following sub-maximal exertion at 24-48h delay; consistent with ME/CFS PEM. Not normal fatigue.

nodes.label: Sans Not Subjective Vision Symptom
nodes.description: SANS risk requires objective optic findings (imaging/OCT/fundoscopy), not subjective visual symptoms alone.

nodes.label: SANS Risk Branch
nodes.description: Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome risk indicators: ICP elevation, optic disc edema, globe flattening.

nodes.label: A Urine Biomarker Rarely Explains Fatigue Cognition Alone

nodes.label: The Trier Social Stress Test: Principles and practice
description: The TSST is a valid and reliable acute stressor for experimental human research.
The protocol combines interview-style presentation and surprise mental arithmetic in front of a non-reinforcing panel.
TSST responses vary by sex, age, genotype, disorder status, and protocol features.
The paradigm helps study neurobiology of acute stress under controlled conditions.

nodes.label: Allostatic Load Measurement: A Systematic Review of Reviews, Database Inventory, and Considerations for Neighborhood Research
description: Allostatic load is commonly operationalized through multi-system biomarker composites rather than a single marker.
Measurement approaches vary substantially across studies and databases.
Environmental and neighborhood stressors are relevant contexts for allostatic load research.
Earlier-life allostatic load can be relevant to later health risk, but operationalization must be explicit.

nodes.label: The effect of acute sleep deprivation on cortisol level: a systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Overall cortisol differences after acute sleep deprivation were not significant in pooled crossover studies or RCTs.
Serum cortisol subgroup analysis showed higher cortisol after acute sleep deprivation.
Multiple cortisol measurements showed significant pooled effects, while single assessments did not.
The sleep deprivation-cortisol relationship is mixed and method-dependent.

nodes.label: The Trier Social Stress Test as a paradigm to study how people respond to threat in social interactions
description: Social-evaluative threat reliably elicits neurophysiological stress reactions in TSST contexts.
The TSST is adaptable for studying social interaction, social support, cognition, and behavior under threat.
Social variables can amplify, buffer, or be affected by stress responses.
Cortisol is a major but not exclusive marker of TSST-related stress response.

nodes.label: A meta-analysis of salivary cortisol responses in the Trier Social Stress Test to evaluate the effects of speech topics, sex, and sample size
description: The TSST is a widely used laboratory paradigm for psychosocial stress induction.
Salivary cortisol reactivity is usable as an objective HPA-axis marker in TSST contexts.
Cortisol responses vary by sex and protocol features, so context and sampling design must be represented explicitly.
Baseline and peak salivary cortisol values should be interpreted separately rather than collapsed into a generic stress score.

nodes.label: Allostasis and allostatic load: implications for neuropsychopharmacology
description: Allostasis describes adaptation through physiological change rather than fixed homeostasis.
Stress mediators such as glucocorticoids and catecholamines are protective in the short term but costly when repeatedly or chronically activated.
Allostatic load links stress biology, brain function, systemic disease risk, and psychiatric vulnerability.
The brain is both an interpreter of environmental challenge and a target of stress mediators.

nodes.label: Physiology and Neurobiology of Stress and Adaptation: Central Role of the Brain
description: The brain is central in perceiving stressors, coordinating responses, and being changed by stress exposure.
Stress adaptation involves multiple interacting systems rather than one biomarker.
Repeated or dysregulated stress responses can contribute to allostatic load.
Stress effects are context-dependent and can be adaptive or damaging depending on timing and duration.

nodes.label: Sleep loss and emotion: A systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Sleep loss affects emotional functioning across multiple outcome types.
Sleep loss can reduce positive affect and alter negative emotional responses.
Effects vary by type of sleep loss and emotional outcome.
The evidence supports sleep loss as a modifier of psychological-state vulnerability, not a diagnostic signal.

nodes.label: An Overview of Heart Rate Variability Metrics and Norms
description: HRV metrics differ in physiological meaning and should not be treated as interchangeable.
HRV is a non-invasive marker of autonomic regulation, especially when interpreted relative to protocol and baseline.
Recording duration, respiration, age, fitness, health status, medication, and measurement quality affect HRV interpretation.
Ultra-short and short-term HRV measures require caution and are not equivalent to 24-hour recordings.

nodes.label: Beyond EDA: A Systematic Review of Multimodal Sympathetic Nervous System Arousal Classification for Stress Detection
description: EDA is a central signal for sympathetic arousal but is insufficient alone for reliable stress interpretation.
Multimodal signals such as HRV, PPG, skin temperature, movement, and context improve interpretability.
Stress detection must distinguish arousal from cognitive load, movement, thermoregulation, excitement, and other confounds.
Signal quality and context are necessary for wearable-based SNS inference.

nodes.label: The effect of sleep deprivation and restriction on mood, emotion, and emotion regulation: three meta-analyses in one
description: Sleep loss moderately increases negative mood states.
Sleep loss has a large diminishing effect on positive mood states.
Sleep loss has a modest blunting effect on emotional arousal.
Sleep restriction shows a small negative effect on adaptive emotion regulation in available youth studies.

nodes.label: Emotion, emotion regulation and sleep: An intimate relationship
description: Sleep and emotion have a bidirectional relationship.
Sleep loss can increase emotional arousal and sensitivity to stressful stimuli.
Sleep quality and amount influence how people react to emotional events.
Emotion regulation may modulate the effect of emotional stress on sleep.

nodes.label: Generalizable Machine Learning for Stress Monitoring from Wearable Devices: A Systematic Literature Review
description: Wearable features can support stress monitoring but generalization remains difficult.
Models often depend on limited datasets, heterogeneous protocols, and weak external validation.
Subject-independent performance and reproducibility are central challenges.
Personal baseline and uncertainty handling are necessary for responsible inference.

nodes.label: Heart rate variability in mental disorders: an umbrella review of meta-analyses
description: HRV is widely studied in mental disorders but remains controversial and heterogeneous.
Mental disorders can be associated with altered autonomic regulation as reflected in HRV.
HRV findings are not disorder-specific enough for direct diagnosis.
Treatment-related HRV changes require cautious interpretation due to differences across metrics and populations.

nodes.label: Heart rate variability status at rest in adult depressed patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Resting HRV can differ between depressed patients and healthy controls at the group level.
The finding supports a link between depression and autonomic regulation abnormalities.
HRV remains non-specific and cannot be used as an individual depression detector.
Medication, comorbidity, HRV metric type, and population characteristics can affect interpretation.

nodes.label: High intradaily variability, low interdaily stability, low relative amplitude, fragmented activity
nodes.description: Disrupted rest-activity organization; can occur with aging, mood disorders, cancer/fatigue states, sleep disruption, or environmental schedule constraints.

guardrail_texts: Activity fragmentation is not specific to fatigue.
Rest-activity rhythm is influenced by schedule/environment, not only internal circadian state.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Fragmented activity plus fragmented sleep supports disrupted rest-activity recovery branch.
Rest-activity disruption plus low HRV suggests low recovery but is not mechanism-specific.

nodes.label: abnormal acylcarnitine profile with reduced fat oxidation/exercise intolerance
nodes.description: possible impaired substrate utilization or metabolic flexibility

guardrail_texts: metabolomics pattern alone does not diagnose mitochondrial disease

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: abnormal acylcarnitines + reduced fat oxidation + exercise intolerance supports impaired metabolic flexibility branch

nodes.label: Low albumin/prealbumin/total protein
nodes.description: May reflect malnutrition, inflammation/illness, liver disease, kidney loss, or fluid shifts depending on context.

guardrail_texts: Albumin/prealbumin are not pure nutrition markers.
Interpret with inflammation, fluid shifts, liver and kidney context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low albumin/prealbumin with inflammation supports illness/inflammation-nutrition confounded branch.
Low serum protein plus urinary protein loss supports renal protein loss branch.

nodes.label: Borderline/low B12 with elevated MMA and/or homocysteine
nodes.description: Suggests functional B12 deficiency more strongly than serum B12 alone.

guardrail_texts: Serum B12 alone is insufficient to diagnose functional B12 deficiency.
Interpret MMA with renal function.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Macrocytosis plus abnormal B12/MMA/homocysteine supports megaloblastic deficiency branch.
Symptoms increase clinical relevance but do not prove causality without biochemical pattern.

nodes.label: Low folate and/or B12 with macrocytosis
nodes.description: Supports megaloblastic deficiency pattern when paired with CBC changes.

guardrail_texts: Do not treat macrocytosis as B12 deficiency without folate, medication, alcohol/liver and thyroid context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: GI malabsorption plus B-vitamin deficiency supports malabsorption-fatigue branch.

nodes.label: elevated BHB/ketones
nodes.description: ketone utilization state; meaning depends on glucose, intake, body mass, symptoms

guardrail_texts: do not infer low energy availability from ketones alone
separate blood ketones from urine ketones

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: high BHB + low intake + weight loss + fatigue suggests low energy availability rather than nutritional ketosis

nodes.label: Bristol 1-2/low frequency or Bristol 6-7/high frequency
nodes.description: phenotype marker for transit/stool pattern, not mechanism

guardrail_texts: Do not infer dysbiosis from stool pattern alone.
Bristol pattern is phenotype, not diagnosis.
Do not infer dehydration without urine/blood/intake/context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: high-frequency diarrhea + concentrated urine suggests dehydration risk branch

nodes.label: high glycemic variability / low time in range
nodes.description: in diabetes contexts predicts higher complication risk independent of HbA1c

guardrail_texts: acute symptom inference requires time-locked CGM + symptoms/cognition

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: needed to test acute postprandial symptom branch

nodes.label: above reference range without imaging evidence
nodes.description: coagulation activation/fibrinolysis; nonspecific

guardrail_texts: do not infer thrombosis from D-dimer alone

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: high D-dimer + symptoms + positive imaging supports thrombotic event branch

nodes.label: Low daytime light exposure and/or high evening blue/melanopic light exposure
nodes.description: Input pattern that increases risk of delayed circadian phase, later sleep timing, longer sleep onset latency, and altered alertness.

guardrail_texts: Light exposure is input, not circadian phase itself.
Do not compare lux-only data to melanopic light without caution.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low daytime plus high evening light with delayed sleep supports circadian delay branch.
Evening melanopic light plus delayed DLMO strongly supports biological phase delay.

nodes.label: Delayed DLMO or shifted urinary aMT6s acrophase
nodes.description: Biological circadian phase shift; DLMO is a high-value marker of central circadian phase, while urinary aMT6s is useful in field studies.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer sleep quality from melatonin alone.
Urinary aMT6s is a proxy, not direct sleep measurement.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Delayed melatonin phase plus late sleep timing supports delayed circadian phase branch.
Dynamic lighting plus stabilized aMT6s supports circadian countermeasure response.

nodes.label: increased fasting glucose / impaired fasting glucose / diabetes-range pattern
nodes.description: impaired glucose regulation or diabetes risk

guardrail_texts: fasting glucose alone does not describe variability or postprandial excursions

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: adds chronicity context

nodes.label: elevated calprotectin/lactoferrin
nodes.description: intestinal inflammation marker; helps distinguish inflammatory from functional patterns

guardrail_texts: Do not infer IBD from calprotectin alone.
Fecal inflammation markers can rise with infection, NSAIDs, and other GI inflammation.
Normal marker does not erase symptoms.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: fecal inflammation + systemic inflammation + GI symptoms supports intestinal inflammation/IBD branch

nodes.label: positive FIT/FOBT
nodes.description: blood detected in stool; useful screening/triage marker

guardrail_texts: Do not infer source of bleeding from FIT/FOBT alone.
Positive FIT/FOBT is not a diagnosis; requires clinical follow-up.
False positives and non-neoplastic bleeding are possible.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: positive stool blood + low Hb/ferritin supports GI blood loss -> iron deficiency branch

nodes.label: elevated HbA1c
nodes.description: chronic average glycemic exposure

guardrail_texts: HbA1c does not measure glycemic variability

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: CGM adds variability not captured by HbA1c

nodes.label: viral DNA/serologic evidence of herpesvirus reactivation with stress/depression context
nodes.description: population-level association with stress/depression; not active symptomatic infection by itself

guardrail_texts: viral DNA alone does not prove active symptomatic infection
do not infer fatigue without timing/context

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: HHV reactivation + stress/depression supports stress-related viral reactivation branch

nodes.label: shifted melatonin metabolite or elevated stress hormone metabolites
nodes.description: non-invasive proxy for circadian output or stress physiology

guardrail_texts: Urine hormone metabolites are not equivalent to serum/saliva levels.
6-sulfatoxymelatonin is not direct sleep quality.
Timing/collection protocol is mandatory.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: shifted 6-sulfatoxymelatonin + light/sleep timing supports circadian disruption branch

nodes.label: flattened diurnal cortisol slope / disrupted rhythm
nodes.description: associated with poorer health/stress-circadian disruption; rhythm more meaningful than one value

guardrail_texts: do not infer HPA-axis state from one cortisol value

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: supports HPA-circadian disruption branch

nodes.label: high fasting insulin/HOMA-IR/TyG/TG-HDL/METS-IR
nodes.description: insulin resistance/metabolic syndrome risk

guardrail_texts: TyG/TG-HDL alone should not diagnose beta-cell dysfunction

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: high TyG + high BMI/WC improves metabolic syndrome/hypertension risk inference

nodes.label: Low ferritin and/or low TSAT with normal CRP/AGP
nodes.description: Iron deficiency likely when inflammatory markers are not elevated.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer anemia from low ferritin alone.
Do not infer oxygen delivery limitation without Hb/Hct/performance context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low Hb + low ferritin/TSAT + low MCV or high RDW supports iron deficiency anemia and oxygen-delivery limitation branch.
Low ferritin/TSAT with fatigue but normal Hb supports iron deficiency without anemia branch, not oxygen-delivery limitation unless performance/HR response supports it.
Iron deficiency with positive occult blood or malabsorption markers supports GI blood loss/malabsorption investigation branch.

nodes.label: Normal/high ferritin with elevated CRP/AGP or inflammatory disease context
nodes.description: Ferritin may be elevated as an acute phase reactant; iron stores may be overestimated.

guardrail_texts: Normal/high ferritin does not rule out iron deficiency when inflammation is present.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Inflammation can mask iron deficiency by raising ferritin; TSAT/sTfR helps disambiguate.

nodes.label: not sufficiently covered in uploaded metabolic files
nodes.description: not sufficiently covered in uploaded metabolic files

guardrail_texts: ketones do not automatically mean pathology
lactate does not automatically mean toxin

nodes.label: high lactate at low/submaximal workload
nodes.description: metabolic strain; possible impaired oxidative metabolism when intensity-adjusted

guardrail_texts: lactate rises normally with exercise intensity
do not infer mitochondrial dysfunction from fatigue alone

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: elevated lactate at low workload + reduced anaerobic threshold + exertional intolerance supports metabolic strain branch

nodes.label: fecal fat high and/or pancreatic elastase low and/or bile acid abnormality
nodes.description: supports malabsorption/digestive insufficiency branch depending on marker pattern

guardrail_texts: Do not infer malabsorption from fatigue alone.
Fecal fat requires dietary/context interpretation.
Bile acid branch requires chronic watery diarrhea context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: malabsorption markers + nutrient deficiency supports malabsorption-fatigue cluster

nodes.label: decreased alpha diversity or altered beta diversity
nodes.description: associated with several disease states but not diagnostic

guardrail_texts: Low diversity is not always pathological.
Do not infer dysbiosis/disease from diversity alone.
Associations are often cross-sectional.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: lower diversity + inflammatory/metabolic markers supports inflammatory/metabolic microbiome disturbance hypothesis

nodes.label: Reduced N3 percent and/or reduced EEG slow-wave activity with normal or near-normal total sleep time
nodes.description: May indicate impaired restorative sleep architecture; interpretation requires cognition/fatigue outcomes.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer N3 impairment from consumer wearable staging.
Do not infer cognition mechanism without measured outcome.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low N3/SWA plus memory/fatigue supports sleep architecture recovery branch.

nodes.label: catabolic nitrogen balance pattern
nodes.description: protein metabolism/catabolism indicator when intake and excretion are quantified

guardrail_texts: Nitrogen balance is not muscle loss by itself.
Requires accurate intake and collection data.
Single measurement is not stable phenotype.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: negative nitrogen balance + low energy/protein intake + muscle loss supports catabolic/low-energy availability branch

nodes.label: increased periodontal pathogens with oral inflammatory signs
nodes.description: local oral inflammatory disease risk when confirmed clinically

guardrail_texts: do not infer gut dysbiosis from oral microbiome
do not treat oral microbiome shift as diagnosis

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: oral dysbiosis + periodontitis findings supports local periodontal inflammatory branch

nodes.label: elevated urinary oxidative stress markers
nodes.description: non-specific oxidative stress signal

guardrail_texts: Oxidative stress marker lacks mechanism specificity.
Do not explain fatigue from urine oxidative marker alone.
Repeat and cross-modal context needed.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: oxidative marker + radiation/inflammation + symptoms supports oxidative stress hypothesis

nodes.label: Elevated periodic limb movement index with arousals or sleep fragmentation
nodes.description: May contribute to sleep fragmentation and daytime sleepiness when temporally linked.

guardrail_texts: Do not attribute daytime symptoms to limb movements without arousal/fragmentation and symptom timing.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: PLMI plus iron context may guide restless legs/limb movement interpretation.
PLMI plus fragmentation plus daytime sleepiness supports limb movement sleep fragmentation branch.

nodes.label: High exertion/load followed by delayed activity reduction and symptom worsening
nodes.description: Candidate PEM/activity-pacing signature only when temporally linked to symptom worsening.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer PEM without delayed symptom/activity pattern.
One low-activity day is not sufficient.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Delayed symptom worsening plus activity drop supports post-exertional malaise activity branch.
Activity drop plus metabolic strain markers strengthens exertional intolerance branch.

nodes.label: Reduced or fragmented REM sleep, abnormal REM latency, or REM shift relative to baseline
nodes.description: REM changes are context-dependent and should not be treated as mood/cognition mechanism without linked outcomes.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer mood mechanism from REM changes without measured mood/circadian outcomes.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: REM disruption plus mood change and circadian misalignment supports REM mood circadian branch.

nodes.label: Irregular rest-activity rhythm, social jetlag, shifted sleep midpoint, low interdaily stability
nodes.description: Behavioral/circadian proxy of misalignment, but affected by schedule, social constraints, illness, and environment.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer circadian phase from bedtime alone.
Rest-activity rhythms are influenced by non-circadian factors.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Misalignment plus reduced sleep and PVT lapses supports alertness performance risk branch.

nodes.label: rapid rise during/after psychosocial stress
nodes.description: sympathetic/adrenomedullary activation proxy

guardrail_texts: sAA is not HPA-axis cortisol
not interchangeable with catecholamines

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: sAA rise + tachycardia/low HRV supports sympathetic arousal branch

nodes.label: flattened slope or blunted cortisol awakening response
nodes.description: altered HPA/circadian regulation; context-dependent

guardrail_texts: one saliva sample cannot infer HPA-axis disorder
total cortisol AUC rarely tracks fatigue strongly

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: flattened slope/CAR + poor sleep + fatigue/mood symptoms supports HPA-circadian disruption branch

nodes.label: low sIgA relative to baseline with high workload/stress and URI episodes
nodes.description: mucosal immune vulnerability branch

guardrail_texts: do not infer systemic immune suppression from saliva alone

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: low sIgA + frequent URI + high workload/stress supports mucosal immune vulnerability

nodes.label: low sIgA with stress/infection; elevated IL-6/TNF/IL-1β with stress/sleep changes
nodes.description: mucosal immune vulnerability or local inflammatory activation

guardrail_texts: do not infer systemic inflammation from saliva cytokines alone

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: low sIgA + high stress/training load + URTI episodes supports mucosal immune suppression hypothesis

nodes.label: High total sedentary time and/or prolonged sedentary bouts
nodes.description: Behavioral inactivity pattern associated with adverse health outcomes in pooled accelerometry research.

guardrail_texts: Separate sedentary behavior from sleep.
Do not infer deconditioning from sedentary time alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: High sedentary time with normal sleep points toward behavioral inactivity rather than sleep extension.
Sedentary pattern plus mood symptoms may reflect behavioral low-recovery cluster but is not diagnostic.

nodes.label: phase-dependent or contraception-modulated hormone pattern
nodes.description: may modulate mood/sleep/HRV/cognition/HPA response

guardrail_texts: do not infer hormone-related mood change without cycle/contraception/timing context

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: supports sex-hormone modulation branch if temporally aligned

nodes.label: Reduced SWS/N3 or altered REM/NREM architecture
nodes.description: Sleep architecture alteration associated with aging, neurodegeneration, memory/performance changes in some literature.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer sleep architecture from consumer wearable staging without validation.
Wearable sleep-stage abnormality without PSG is uncertain.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low SWS/N3 with impaired memory/performance supports sleep architecture cognition branch.

nodes.label: Elevated AHI/RDI with elevated ODI and/or nocturnal oxygen desaturation
nodes.description: Supports objective sleep-disordered breathing when linked to respiratory events; daytime SpO2 may remain normal.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer sleep apnea from fatigue or snoring alone without AHI/RDI/ODI.
Do not infer daytime oxygenation from nocturnal SpO2 alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: SDB markers plus daytime impairment supports sleep-disordered breathing branch.
Nocturnal desaturation with normal daytime SpO2 supports nocturnal respiratory sleep risk branch.

nodes.label: Low sleep efficiency, high WASO, frequent awakenings, high fragmentation
nodes.description: Disrupted sleep continuity; may impair recovery even if total sleep duration appears adequate.

guardrail_texts: Poor sleep is not a complete fatigue mechanism without context.
Compare devices/protocols cautiously.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Fragmented sleep plus low HRV and fatigue supports poor recovery fragmentation branch.

nodes.label: High WASO and/or arousal index with normal or near-normal total sleep time
nodes.description: Suggests disrupted sleep continuity; may impair recovery even when total sleep duration appears adequate.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer recovery from total sleep time alone.
Check pain, noise, CO2, temperature, medication and circadian phase.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: High fragmentation plus next-day impairment supports sleep fragmentation recovery failure branch.
Night noise plus arousal index plus next-day fatigue supports noise sleep recovery branch.

nodes.label: Below population/personal norm or declining trend over time
nodes.description: Objective reduction in movement volume; may reflect inactivity, deconditioning, illness, injury, mood, confinement, or mission constraints.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer fatigue from low activity alone.
Do not compare Earth step count with microgravity movement without mission context.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low activity plus low VO2 supports a deconditioning branch more than fatigue alone.
Low activity plus strength decline supports functional deconditioning.

nodes.label: reduced butyrate/SCFA or altered bile acid/tryptophan pathway
nodes.description: microbiome functional/metabolite signal; systemic meaning requires cross-modal data

guardrail_texts: SCFA concentration is not the same as production/absorption.
Stool metabolite level is not necessarily systemic exposure.
Do not infer causality from cross-sectional metabolomics.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: stool tryptophan/kynurenine shift + inflammation + mood/fatigue supports immune-metabolic gut-brain hypothesis

nodes.label: pathogen/toxin positive with compatible symptoms
nodes.description: supports infectious diarrhea branch when symptoms and timing fit

guardrail_texts: Separate colonization from active disease when tests allow.
Do not infer dehydration from diarrhea alone.
Do not conflate infectious diarrhea with IBD/IBS.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: infectious diarrhea + concentrated urine/electrolyte shift supports dehydration risk branch

nodes.label: Poor subjective sleep with normal PSG architecture and normal objective sleep continuity
nodes.description: Suggests symptom perception, anxiety, pain, environmental or insomnia-cognitive context rather than objectively disrupted architecture.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer insomnia mechanism from subjective sleep complaint alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Poor subjective sleep with normal PSG should trigger context override search.

nodes.label: Poor subjective sleep quality with normal objective TST/efficiency
nodes.description: Subjective-objective sleep mismatch; common in insomnia and psychiatric/stress states.

guardrail_texts: Subjective and objective sleep metrics are not equivalent.
Do not dismiss subjective distress as unreal.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Poor subjective sleep with normal objective metrics plus stress/anxiety supports sleep perception mismatch branch.

nodes.label: butyrate-producer depletion or pro-inflammatory taxa expansion
nodes.description: taxa-level association with gut/metabolic/immune states; not diagnostic alone

guardrail_texts: Do not infer disease from a single taxon.
Probiotic-associated taxa increase is not automatically beneficial.
Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes ratio is not reliable standalone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: low Faecalibacterium/butyrate producers + CRP/cytokines + GI symptoms supports intestinal inflammation-metabolite branch

nodes.label: TSH low + free T4/free T3 high
nodes.description: overt hyperthyroid pattern

guardrail_texts: do not infer psychiatric anxiety from tachycardia before endocrine/autonomic/medication context

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: endocrine-like anxiety/tachycardia branch

nodes.label: TSH high + free T4 low
nodes.description: overt hypothyroidism pattern

guardrail_texts: do not infer thyroid dysfunction from fatigue alone

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: supports hypothyroid physiological phenotype when labs are abnormal

nodes.label: TSH high + free T4 normal
nodes.description: subclinical hypothyroid lab pattern

guardrail_texts: subclinical pattern is not overt dysfunction

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: antibody positivity increases future/overt dysfunction risk

nodes.label: <7h TST or significant within-person reduction from baseline/control group
nodes.description: Short TST alone does not define poor sleep quality or recovery.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer sleep recovery from total sleep time alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Short TST plus next-day lapses supports acute sleep loss branch.

nodes.label: Below recommended or age-specific total sleep time; often <6h in adults
nodes.description: Short sleep is associated with impaired alertness/performance and multiple adverse health outcomes, but cannot alone define recovery state.

guardrail_texts: Separate sleep opportunity from actual sleep duration.
Do not infer recovery state from sleep duration alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Short TST plus PVT lapses and sleepiness supports sleep-loss alertness impairment branch.

nodes.label: elevated urinary calcium/mineral excretion in unloading/microgravity context
nodes.description: possible mineral mobilization signal; not bone loss by itself

guardrail_texts: Urinary calcium alone != bone loss.
Separate bone loss risk from kidney stone risk/current stone.
Diet and exercise countermeasures must be included.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: urinary calcium + bone turnover/DXA + microgravity supports bone mineral loss risk branch

nodes.label: elevated tubular injury/dysfunction markers
nodes.description: renal tubular injury/dysfunction signal; associated with frailty/cognitive impairment in CKD contexts

guardrail_texts: Do not generalize CKD findings to healthy astronauts without validation.
Cutoffs vary; repeat/context required.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: tubular markers + frailty/cognitive impairment in CKD supports renal-systemic vulnerability branch

nodes.label: high UOsm/USG, dark color, low volume
nodes.description: concentrated urine and renal water conservation; not sufficient for clinical dehydration

guardrail_texts: Concentrated urine alone does not confirm clinically meaningful dehydration.
Hydration marker is not blood volume/orthostatic tolerance.
Accuracy is weaker in older/comorbid populations.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: concentrated urine + normal plasma osmolality supports renal conservation adaptation
concentrated urine + orthostatic tachycardia/hypotension + dizziness supports hypovolemia/dehydration hypothesis

nodes.label: multi-metabolite pattern discriminating mood/cognitive/fatigue-related groups
nodes.description: research-level classifier signal; may separate group-level disease states

guardrail_texts: Single-day urine metabolomics does not represent stable phenotype.
Group classifier is not individual diagnosis.
External validation required.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: repeated urine metabolomics + timed symptoms/cognitive tests may support personalized trajectory tracking

nodes.label: Low 25OH vitamin D with elevated PTH and bone/mineral context
nodes.description: Suggests vitamin D-related bone-mineral stress when paired with PTH/calcium/phosphate and bone context.

guardrail_texts: Vitamin D deficiency alone should not explain fatigue.
Do not infer bone loss from vitamin D/calcium alone.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low vitamin D/high PTH plus urinary calcium/bone turnover and microgravity supports bone mineral risk branch.
Bone markers are required to move from nutrient abnormality to bone loss risk.

nodes.label: Low or high serum magnesium
nodes.description: Serum magnesium may reflect circulating magnesium but is not a complete measure of total body magnesium status.

guardrail_texts: Serum magnesium is not equivalent to total body magnesium stores.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Serum plus urine magnesium helps separate intake/renal loss/GI loss contexts.

nodes.label: elevated vWF/Factor VIII with CRP/IL6/NLR/endothelial markers
nodes.description: endothelial activation/dysfunction context

guardrail_texts: endothelial markers are not direct perfusion measurements

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: high vWF/FVIII + inflammation supports endothelial activation branch

nodes.label: Wearable shows normal sleep duration/stage distribution while PSG shows high arousals, low N3, or SDB
nodes.description: Consumer wearable estimates can miss fragmentation, arousals, deep sleep loss or sleep-disordered breathing.

guardrail_texts: Do not infer PSG stages from consumer wearable staging.
Do not use consumer wearable staging to diagnose specific sleep disorders.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Normal wearable sleep with abnormal PSG supports wearable false reassurance branch.

nodes.label: Low zinc, copper, or selenium, alone or patterned
nodes.description: May reflect micronutrient insufficiency, inflammation redistribution, or dietary/malabsorption context.

guardrail_texts: Trace mineral abnormalities are context dependent and affected by inflammation and protein status.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low trace minerals with immune alterations may support micronutrient-immune function hypothesis but rarely a standalone explanation.
